title: B1 Listening Part 1 - Test 23
instructions: For each question, listen to the short recording and choose the correct picture: A, B or C.

questions.context: You hear an announcement for passengers travelling to Manchester.
questions.audio_script: Speaker: Good afternoon. This is a message for passengers travelling to Manchester. You may have seen the earlier notice asking you to wait near the bus stop seven, but that information has changed. [1]Please go to the new shelter now[/1]. Do not use the old shelter, because the old roof is damaged. Staff will be nearby if you need help with luggage or directions. Please move carefully and keep your tickets ready for checking. Thank you for your patience today. || Speaker: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Good afternoon. This is a message for passengers travelling to Manchester. You may have seen the earlier notice asking you to wait near the bus stop seven, but that information has changed. Please go to the new shelter now. Do not use the old shelter, because the old roof is damaged. Staff will be nearby if you need help with luggage or directions. Please move carefully and keep your tickets ready for checking. Thank you for your patience today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.question: Where should passengers go now?

A.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people waiting under an old shelter, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

B.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people waiting at a new bus shelter, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

C.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, people standing by a shop window, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

questions.explanation: The announcement says passengers should go to the new shelter now.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a broken camera.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Did anyone fix your camera? || Man: Yes, but it wasn’t the problem I expected. I thought it was the memory card, because it looked odd yesterday. || Woman: Was it the button then? || Man: I wondered that too, because the button felt loose. Then someone checked it carefully and [1]the camera worked again after she charged the battery[/1]. || Woman: So it was quite simple. || Man: Yes, luckily. I need it tonight, so I’m glad it is working again. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did anyone fix your camera?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but it wasn’t the problem I expected. I thought it was the memory card, because it looked odd yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was it the button then?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I wondered that too, because the button felt loose. Then someone checked it carefully and the camera worked again after she charged the battery.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So it was quite simple.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, luckily. I need it tonight, so I’m glad it is working again.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.question: What was the problem with the camera?

A.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, camera memory card, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

B.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, camera battery on a charger, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

C.imagePrompt: Simple Cambridge exam illustration style, camera button, no text or labels, flat educational drawing, black and white line art, minimal details, clean white background.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the camera worked again after she charged the battery, so option B is correct.

questions.context: You hear two people choosing food at a school canteen.
questions.audio_script: Woman: Have you chosen what to eat at the school canteen? The queue is moving quickly. || Boy: I nearly chose the fish pie, but fish is finished. || Woman: What about the salad? It looks popular today. || Boy: I thought about it, but salad is too small for lunch. [1]I’m going to have the omelette[/1]. It should be enough before our journey. || Woman: Good choice. I’ll order mine after you, and then we can find a table near the window. || Woman: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you chosen what to eat at the school canteen? The queue is moving quickly.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I nearly chose the fish pie, but fish is finished.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What about the salad? It looks popular today.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I thought about it, but salad is too small for lunch. I’m going to have the omelette. It should be enough before our journey.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good choice. I’ll order mine after you, and then we can find a table near the window.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two friends making plans after school.
questions.audio_script: Boy: Are we still going to the football match after school? || Girl: I don't think so. My ankle still hurts. || Boy: We could try the library instead. || Girl: Maybe, but it closes before we can get there. My aunt has free seats, so [1]we're going to the ice rink this afternoon instead[/1]. || Boy: That sounds fine. Shall we meet outside the bus station? || Girl: Yes, at four. Bring your student card, because they sometimes ask for it at the desk. || Girl: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a present for grandma.
questions.audio_script: Man: I still need a present for grandma. I thought about buying chocolates, but that didn't seem special. || Girl: What about a phone case? You mentioned that last week. || Man: I checked, but the shop only had expensive ones. In the end, [1]I bought a photo frame because she likes family pictures[/1]. || Girl: That sounds perfect. Useful presents are often better than decorations. || Man: I hope so. I’ll wrap it tonight after dinner, and take it over tomorrow. || Man: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

questions.context: You hear two people talking about travelling to the airport.
questions.audio_script: Woman: How are you getting to the airport on Saturday? || Man: I was going to take the coach, but the early coach is full. || Woman: What about the train? || Man: True, but there are engineering works. My brother can lend me money, but [1]I’m going to go by car because my uncle is driving there anyway[/1]. || Woman: Good idea. I’ll meet you by the main entrance at ten, so we can go in together. Don’t forget your ticket. || Woman: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: woman

questions.context: You hear two people talking about a work shift at a library.
questions.audio_script: Man: Are you working at the library this weekend? || Woman: Yes, but not on Tuesday after school as usual. The manager changed the rota because there is a staff meeting. || Man: So when are you going in? || Woman: I can’t do Wednesday morning, and Thursday afternoon is already covered, so [1]I’m doing the Wednesday evening shift[/1]. || Man: That’s not too bad. You’ll still have some free time later. || Woman: Exactly. I can finish my homework then. || Woman: Let's check the plan once more before we leave, just to be sure.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you working at the library this weekend?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but not on Tuesday after school as usual. The manager changed the rota because there is a staff meeting.

dialogue.speaker: man

dialogue.text: I can’t do Wednesday morning, and Thursday afternoon is already covered, so I’m doing the Wednesday evening shift.
